title: Ash Creek Route
briefing: You have been called in to investigate the disappearance of Lena Mercer, a 29-year-old rural mail carrier for Cottonwood County. Lena failed to return from Route 17A on the evening of September 14th. Her postal vehicle was found abandoned at a turnout near Ash Creek with mail tubs still inside, her wallet on the seat, and no sign of voluntary departure. Your job is to determine what happened to Lena, identify who is responsible, and build enough evidence to make an accusation. You have a limited investigation budget — spend it wisely.
solution: Lena Mercer was abducted by Calvin Rusk, owner of Rusk Recovery & Salvage. During her rural mail route she noticed Rusk using the closed Ash Creek weigh station near Mile Marker 12 to offload stolen farm equipment. Rusk intercepted her, removed the SD card from the postal truck dashcam, forced her into his rollback tow truck, and hid her alive inside a locked parts-storage container at his salvage yard. The case is solved by linking the last scanner ping, orange clay transfer, gas station video, the empty dashcam slot, and Rusk's false poker alibi.

suspects.name: Noah Pike
suspects.alibi: Working late shift at Pike Auto Parts from 6:00 PM to 10:15 PM
suspects.motive: Jealousy over the breakup and unresolved debt Lena refused to cover
what_they_know: Lena said she was scared by something she saw near Ash Creek earlier that week
Lena had recently started saving screenshots and notes about unusual vehicles on her route
what_they_lie_about: He claims he had no contact with Lena that day, but he called her twice before noon
He downplays how angry he was about her refusing to lend him money

suspects.name: Dana Blevins
suspects.alibi: At the county mail annex handling sort errors and dispatch logs until after 8:00 PM
suspects.motive: Lena had threatened to file a safety complaint about route changes Dana approved without inspection
what_they_know: Lena reported a tow truck parked near the closed weigh station two days before she vanished
Dana redirected Lena to cover the Ash Creek stretch because another carrier called in sick
what_they_lie_about: Dana says Lena never raised a safety concern about the route
Dana omits that she told Lena to finish the route instead of returning early

suspects.name: Elias Voss
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Route customer and local drone hobbyist
suspects.alibi: Flying his drone near the gravel pit west of the creek around sundown
suspects.motive: Unwanted fascination with Lena and resentment after she told him to stop waiting for her on route days
what_they_know: He saw a white rollback truck on County Road 8 shortly after Lena's postal vehicle passed
He noticed Lena pulled over near Mile Marker 12 with her driver door open
what_they_lie_about: He first says he was nowhere near Lena's route that evening
He hides that he followed her truck with his drone for several minutes

suspects.name: Calvin Rusk
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Owner of Rusk Recovery & Salvage; occasional route customer
suspects.alibi: Playing cards at the Rusted Rail Bar from 6:30 PM to 9:30 PM
suspects.motive: Lena witnessed him offloading stolen farm equipment and he abducted her to silence a witness and avoid a probation violation
what_they_know: He removed the dashcam SD card from Lena's postal truck
He moved Lena from his tow truck to a locked storage container at his salvage yard
He left his phone at the bar with an associate to support a false alibi
what_they_lie_about: He says he was never near Ash Creek that evening
He says his rollback truck was out of service due to a hydraulic leak
He denies knowing Lena beyond routine mail deliveries

timeline.event: Lena begins Route 17A and signs out vehicle RM-214
Lena tells Noah by phone that she may report something strange she has been seeing near the closed Ash Creek weigh station
Dana instructs Lena to cover the Ash Creek stretch because another carrier called in sick
Lena scans a certified envelope at the Harlow farm mailbox
Elias sees Lena's postal truck stopped near Mile Marker 12 with a white rollback truck approaching from behind
Last known scanner ping from Lena's route device
Unsent draft text created on Lena's phone: 'Tow truck again. If I do not come back check mile 12'
Gas station camera captures Calvin Rusk's rollback truck traveling east toward Ash Creek
Rusk's phone appears active at the Rusted Rail Bar while his truck is later seen entering the salvage yard
timeline.location: Rusted Rail Bar and Rusk Recovery & Salvage
timeline.source: Cell record and distant camera review

timeline.event: Lena is reported overdue when RM-214 is found abandoned with mail tubs intact
Investigators discover orange clay, a missing dashcam SD card, and strap marks on the postal truck bumper
Warrant team locates Lena alive inside a locked parts-storage container behind Bay 3 at Rusk Recovery & Salvage

evidence.description: Route scanner shows the final ping at the closed Ash Creek weigh station at 6:23 PM
evidence.points_to: The disappearance occurred on-route near Mile Marker 12, not after Lena voluntarily left work

evidence.description: Orange clay and salvage-yard metal dust found on the driver's step and rear bumper of RM-214
evidence.points_to: Contact with a vehicle or location consistent with Rusk Recovery & Salvage

evidence.description: QuickFuel security clip shows Calvin Rusk's white rollback truck heading toward Ash Creek minutes before the last scanner ping
Postal truck dashcam slot is empty and shows recent tool marks where the SD card was removed
evidence.points_to: Evidence was deliberately taken to conceal contact at the scene

evidence.description: Unsent phone draft reads: 'Tow truck again. If I do not come back check mile 12'
evidence.points_to: Lena feared a specific recurring tow truck and anticipated danger

evidence.description: Elias Voss reports seeing Lena stopped with a white rollback truck approaching shortly before sunset
evidence.points_to: A tow truck encounter at the disappearance window

evidence.description: Rusk's phone remained active at the Rusted Rail Bar while another device linked to his truck moved toward the salvage yard
Parallel abrasion marks and strap fibers on the postal truck bumper match the commercial ratchet straps used on rollback tow trucks
evidence.points_to: Victim or equipment was transferred using a rollback truck

evidence.description: Recovered dashcam SD card is found in a burn barrel behind Bay 3; partial footage shows Rusk's truck entering the turnout
Lena is located alive inside a locked parts-storage container with duct tape residue and mail tray plastic nearby
evidence.points_to: Victim was concealed at Rusk Recovery & Salvage

documents.content: Reporting party: Dana Blevins. Subject overdue on Route 17A. Postal vehicle located at Ash Creek turnout. Wallet and route tubs present. No sign of voluntary departure.
Noah Pike states Lena was avoiding him and did not mention any danger. Call log later contradicts part of that claim and shows two calls with Lena before noon.
Dana to Lena at 3:55 PM: Need you to finish the Ash Creek stretch today. Stay on schedule and note any road issues after route close.
Elias Voss initially denies being near the route, then admits he saw Lena stopped near Mile Marker 12 and a white rollback truck coming up behind her.
Field exam of RM-214 found orange clay, ferrous dust, and empty dashcam card slot. Pattern suggests deliberate evidence removal rather than routine loss.
Rusted Rail cashier recalls Calvin arriving briefly, leaving his phone on the table, and stepping outside after 6:40 PM. Card tab remained open with another patron.
Search of Rusk Recovery & Salvage located a burn barrel with melted plastic, a damaged SD card, and a locked parts-storage container behind Bay 3 containing the missing victim alive.

narrative.opening_scene: The call comes in at 10:47 PM — RM-214 found abandoned at a dirt turnout near Ash Creek, engine cold, wallet still on the seat. Lena Mercer signed out of the annex at seven-eighteen that morning and was supposed to be back by six. Nobody reported her missing for four hours. Now it's your case.

mysterious_message.message_content: Tow truck again. If I do not come back check mile 12

threads.subject: Route adjustment — today
threads.body: Lena — Darren called in sick. You are covering the Ash Creek stretch today. Finish the full route before returning to the annex. Note any road conditions in the log. Do not come back early.

threads.subject: Stop calling me while I am on route
threads.body: I told you the answer is no on the money. And there is something going on near the Ash Creek stretch that has me nervous. I will call you after I finish today but please do not call while I am driving.

messages.body: Hey can we talk today? About the 400.
Not now. I am on route.
I just need 10 minutes. It is important.
No. I will call you when I am done today.
[DRAFT — UNSENT] Tow truck again. If I do not come back check mile 12

locations.name: Ash Creek Turnout
locations.description: Gravel pulloff on Ash Creek Road where Lena's postal vehicle RM-214 was found abandoned.
locations.ambient_details: Tire tracks from a heavy vehicle. Broken fence post. Orange clay near rear bumper.

locations.name: Closed Ash Creek Weigh Station
locations.description: Abandoned county weigh station near Mile Marker 12. Last known scanner ping from Lena route device.
locations.ambient_details: Fresh tire marks through clay on access road. Padlocked gate. Evidence of recent vehicle activity.

locations.name: Pike Auto Parts
locations.description: Auto parts store on County Road 14. Noah Pike claims to have worked here the evening Lena vanished.
locations.ambient_details: Time-clock punch system. Security camera covers parking lot.

locations.name: Rusted Rail Bar
locations.description: Local bar where Calvin Rusk claims he played cards from 6:30 PM to 9:30 PM on September 14th.
locations.ambient_details: Cash receipts. Staff recalls Rusk's phone on table but cannot confirm Rusk was present all night. Security camera at front entrance.

locations.name: Rusk Recovery & Salvage
locations.description: Salvage yard and towing operation owned by Calvin Rusk. Lena is being held in a locked storage container behind Bay 3.
locations.ambient_details: Gated yard. Multiple containers. White rollback truck parked near Bay 3. Fresh clay tracks matching Ash Creek.

locations.name: QuickFuel on Route 6
locations.description: Gas station with security camera that captured Rusk's rollback truck at 6:31 PM heading east toward Ash Creek.
locations.ambient_details: 30-day video retention. Clear view of eastbound lane.

locations.name: Cottonwood County Mail Annex
locations.description: Postal facility where Lena started her route. Dana Blevins's last message to Lena was logged at 3:55 PM.
locations.ambient_details: Badge-in logs, dispatch records, supervisor message system.

locations.name: County Road 8 / Mile Marker 12
locations.description: Rural stretch where Elias Voss saw Lena's vehicle and a white rollback truck.
locations.ambient_details: No street cameras. Orange clay deposits. Faint skid marks on the shoulder.

verifiable_claims.claim: Working late shift at Pike Auto Parts from 6:00 PM to 10:15 PM on September 14th
verifiable_claims.verification_method: Check Pike Auto Parts time-clock records or staff statements
verifiable_claims.ground_truth: Time-clock records confirm Noah clocked in at 4:45 PM and out at 9:55 PM. Alibi is essentially consistent — he was not at Ash Creek. He omits calling Lena twice before noon.

verifiable_claims.claim: Playing cards at the Rusted Rail Bar from 6:30 PM to 9:30 PM on September 14th
verifiable_claims.verification_method: Check bar for receipts, staff statements, security footage, or review truck location data
verifiable_claims.ground_truth: FALSE. QuickFuel footage shows Rusk's rollback truck on Route 6 at 6:31 PM heading east. His phone was left with an associate at the bar. Bar staff confirm the phone but cannot confirm Rusk was present.

verifiable_claims.claim: Flying his drone near the gravel pit west of the creek around sundown
verifiable_claims.verification_method: Check drone GPS flight logs stored in the controller
verifiable_claims.ground_truth: Drone GPS logs show Elias flying along County Road 8 near Mile Marker 12, not the gravel pit. He was much closer to the incident than he admitted.

verifiable_claims.claim: At the county mail annex handling sort errors and dispatch logs until after 8:00 PM
verifiable_claims.verification_method: Check mail annex badge logs and supervisor system entries
verifiable_claims.ground_truth: Badge logs confirm Dana badged out at 8:17 PM. Dispatcher entries show activity through 8:14 PM. Alibi confirmed.

verifiable_claims.claim: His rollback truck was out of service due to a hydraulic leak on September 14th
verifiable_claims.verification_method: Check repair records or inspect the truck at Rusk Recovery & Salvage
verifiable_claims.ground_truth: FALSE. QuickFuel footage shows the truck operating normally at 6:31 PM. No repair records exist for a hydraulic issue on that date.
